channel.title: EDGAR Live Scanner
channel.description: High-signal SEC EDGAR filings — tender offers, going-private, activist stakes, spinoffs, delistings, busted deals.

item.title: 📑 S-4 — PSA — Public Storage
item.description:
Public Storage (PSA) will acquire National Storage Affiliates Trust (NSA) in a stock-for-stock merger. Each NSA common share will be converted into 0.1400 PSA shares. The merger is subject to NSA shareholder approval and other conditions; a proxy statement has been filed.

  • Counterparty: National Storage Affiliates Trust
  • Offer price: 0.1400 PSA shares per NSA share
  • Consideration: stock

Mkt cap $54.69B · EV $55.46B · Fwd P/E 32.3x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 13.9x · EV/Sales 11.0x · Real Estate / REIT - Industrial

PSA · S-4 · cat=acquisition · filed 2026-05-11

item.title: 📰 Merger comms — MCR — MFS CHARTER INCOME TRUST — [425]
item.description:
abrdn Inc., the proposed investment adviser to MFS Multimarket Income Trust (MMT), announced its intention to propose a future policy amendment requiring an average investment grade credit quality portfolio, subject to shareholder approval. This is part of the previously announced reorganization of MFS Charter Income Trust (MCR) and MFS Intermediate High Income Fund (CIF) into MMT, which remains subject to shareholder votes. The transaction is expected to close after shareholder approvals.

  • Counterparty: MFS Multimarket Income Trust
  • Consideration: stock

Mkt cap $253.5M · EV $358.3M · Financial Services / Asset Management - Income

item.description:

(target) to fund extension fees for the pending business combination under the November 9, 2025 Business Combination Agreement. The note is convertible into private units at $10/unit or into shares at $5/share upon termination. The merger must close by January 22, 2027, with monthly extension options.

item.description:
LGL Group announces a subscription rights offering to raise capital. Each shareholder of record will receive one right per share, each right exercisable for one share of common stock at a subscription price to be determined (expected to be the greater of a 1-5% discount to 30-day VWAP or $6.81 book value per share). The offering is pending SEC effectiveness of a Form S-1 registration statement.

  • Offer price: to be determined
  • Consideration: cash

item.title: 🚪 Delisting notice — AGAE — Allied Gaming & Entertainment Inc.
item.description:
Allied Gaming & Entertainment (AGAE) received a Nasdaq Staff Determination on May 6, 2026, that its common stock will be delisted from the Nasdaq Capital Market due to non-compliance with the minimum bid price rule (below $1.00 for 30 consecutive business days) and failure to file its Annual Report on Form 10-K for 2025. The company intends to request a hearing before an independent panel by May 13, 2026, and is considering a reverse stock split to regain compliance. Trading will continue under the ticker AGAE pending the hearing outcome.

Mkt cap $17.3M · EV $1.1M · EV/Sales 0.0x · Communication Services / Entertainment

AGAE · 8-K · Items 3.01, 5.03, 7.01, 9.01 · cat=delisting · filed 2026-05-11

The business combination was announced on November 9, 2025, and the SPAC has until January 22, 2027 to close.

item.title: ⚔️ Dissident — TWO — TWO HARBORS INVESTMENT CORP. — [DFAN14A]
item.description:
UWM Holdings Corporation (UWMC) increased its acquisition proposal for Two Harbors Investment Corp. (TWO) to $12.50 per share in cash or 2.3328 shares of UWMC stock, urging TWO stockholders to vote against the pending merger with CrossCountry Mortgage (CCM) at the special meeting on May 19, 2026. UWMC claims its offer is clearly superior to CCM's $12.00 per share cash deal and intends to close within approximately two months of signing an agreement. The filing includes an open letter criticizing TWO's board for refusing to engage and for prioritizing management payouts.

  • Counterparty: UWM Holdings Corporation
  • Offer price: $12.50 per share in cash or 2.3328 shares of UWMC stock
  • Consideration: cash or stock election

Mkt cap $1.33B · EV $8.81B · Fwd P/E 11.1x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 2386.8x · EV/Sales 930.5x · Real Estate / REIT - Mortgage

TWO · DFAN14A · cat=activist · filed 2026-05-11

item.title: 📑 Proxy add'l materials — VSCO — Victoria's Secret & Co. — [DEFA14A]
item.description:
Victoria's Secret & Co. filed a supplement to its definitive proxy statement and sent a letter to shareholders responding to a proxy contest by BBRC International Pte Limited and its Chairman Brett Blundy, who seek to oppose the re-election of two board members at the upcoming annual meeting on June 11, 2026. The board urges shareholders to vote for all its nominees, citing strong performance and the risks of disruption. Director Mariam Naficy has decided not to stand for re-election due to the proxy contest.

  • Counterparty: BBRC International Pte Limited

Mkt cap $3.71B · EV $6.88B · Fwd P/E 13.2x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 9.6x · EV/Sales 1.0x · Consumer Cyclical / Apparel - Retail

item.description:

The acquisition is expected to close in Q3 2026 and be immediately accretive, augmenting indie's ADAS and physical AI portfolio.

item.title: 📋 8-K — Enviri II Corp
item.description:
Enviri II Corporation (New Enviri) will be spun off from Enviri Corporation via a pro-rata distribution of one share of New Enviri common stock for every three shares of CE Holdings common stock. The spin-off separates the Harsco Environmental and Harsco Rail segments into a new publicly traded company, while Clean Earth is being sold to Veolia. The SEC declared the Form 10 registration statement effective on May 8, 2026, and the distribution is expected to become effective on June 1, 2026.

UniFirst Corp (UNF) has entered into a definitive merger agreement with Cintas Corporation (CTAS). Under the terms, each UniFirst share will be converted into $155.00 in cash and 0.7720 shares of Cintas common stock. A special shareholder meeting to vote on the merger is scheduled for June 11, 2026, and the Croatti family, controlling approximately two-thirds of voting power, has agreed to vote in favor, making approval likely.

  • Counterparty: Cintas Corporation
  • Offer price: $155.00 cash + 0.7720 CTAS shares per UNF share
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Closing: after June 11, 2026 special meeting and regulatory approvals

item.title: 📋 8-K — MLTX — MoonLake Immunotherapeutics
item.description:
MoonLake Immunotherapeutics announced positive outcome from final pre-BLA meeting with FDA for sonelokimab (SLK) in hidradenitis suppurativa (HS). FDA aligned on submission plans and label strategy, including acceptability of MIRA trial data for effectiveness and inclusion of adolescent data from VELA-TEEN. BLA submission planned for end of September 2026; acceptance and Priority Review decision expected by end of November 2026; first commercial launch targeted for H2 2027 if approved. item.description:

The company is ineligible for a 180-day compliance period because it has effected reverse stock splits over the prior two years with a cumulative ratio of 250 shares or more to one. Aditxt intends to timely request a hearing before a Nasdaq Hearings Panel to appeal the determination, which will stay delisting pending the hearing outcome.

(NASDAQ: GIG), a SPAC, announced shareholder approval of its domestication from Cayman Islands to Delaware, a step toward closing its business combination with Hadron Energy, a developer of small modular nuclear reactors (Halo MMR). item.title: 🚪 Delisting notice — DFNS — T3 Defense Inc.
item.description:
T3 Defense Inc. received a Nasdaq notice on May 5, 2026, for non-compliance with the minimum bid price of $1.00 per share. The company has 180 calendar days, until November 2, 2026, to regain compliance by maintaining a closing bid price of at least $1.00 for 10 consecutive business days. The company may consider a reverse stock split to cure the deficiency.

item.title: ⚔️ Dissident — FRMI — Fermi Inc. — [DFAN14A]
item.description:
Toby Neugebauer, Fermi Inc.'s largest shareholder (~40% stake), has nominated a slate of four independent directors (David Daglio, Charles Elson, John Jimenez, Janet Yang) for election at a special meeting on May 29, 2026. Neugebauer is advocating for a sale or strategic partnership of the company's 'Project Matador' asset, which he describes as having over 2 GW of power generation and ~$1 billion in financing closed. The nominees bring M&A, energy finance, and governance expertise to the board.

  • Counterparty: Toby Neugebauer

item.description:
Mercer International's German subsidiaries entered into a Waiver and Consent Request Letter with lenders to amend their €370.1 million revolving credit facility. The waiver suspends the leverage ratio covenant (max 3.50:1.00) for the first three quarters of fiscal 2026, restricts distributions to the parent (up to €15M aggregate with conditions), caps 2026 capex at €60M, and requires additional security over assets worth at least 110% of drawn amounts. The interest rate margin was modified to 2.50%-4.25% based on leverage levels.

item.description:
Diana Shipping Inc., holder of 14.7% of Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd, is soliciting proxies to elect six director nominees at Genco's 2026 annual meeting on June 18, 2026. The proxy contest aims to replace the board to facilitate a sale or other value-maximizing transaction.

AD Pharma owns 65% of Acura's outstanding common stock.
